articles.headline: 2026 World Cup: Which 48 teams qualified for the tournament?
articles.description: The race to qualify for the 2026 FIFA World Cup is complete. Here's how it all went down across the globe.

images.name: Sweden celebrate [1296x729]
images.credit: Linnea Rheborg - UEFA/UEFA via Getty Images

images.name: Scotland celebrate [1296x729]
images.credit: Alan Harvey/SNS Group via Getty Images

images.name: World Cup trophy New York [1296x729]
images.credit: Mike Stobe - FIFA/FIFA via Getty Images

images.name: Marcotti: Italy have 'no excuses' for another World Cup failure
images.caption: Gab Marcotti reacts to Italy failing to qualify for a third straight World Cup after losing to Bosnia and Herzegovina on penalties.

articles.headline: Could Morocco beat Brazil in Group C at the World Cup?
articles.description: Sam Ogunleye and Ed Dove discuss the strength of Morocco's squad as well as the potential to defeat group favourites Brazil.

images.name: Could Morocco beat Brazil in Group C at the World Cup?
images.caption: Sam Ogunleye and Ed Dove discuss the strength of Morocco's squad as well as the potential to defeat group favourites Brazil.

articles.headline: New Adidas World Cup jerseys rated: Argentina, Mexico, Spain
articles.description: Adidas has released a huge batch of home kits for 22 countries, many of whom have either qualified or still hope to qualify for the FIFA 2026 World Cup.

article.headline: Chelsea's Estêvão scores first goal for Brazil in Chile win
article.description: Chelsea forward Estêvão scored his first goal for Brazil in a 3-0 win over Chile in South American World Cup qualifying.
article.linkText: Paqueta, Guimaraes, Estêvão on target in Brazil win

images.name: Pedro: Playing for Chelsea and Brazil has always been my dream
images.caption: João Pedro says "it's the beginning of a beautiful story" after the 23-year-old earned a call-up to the Brazil squad.

video.headline: Pedro: Playing for Chelsea and Brazil has always been my dream

video.title: Pedro: Playing for Chelsea and Brazil has always been my dream

tracking.trackingName: COM_SOC Presser (Pedro: Playing for Chelsea and Brazil has always been my dream) GLOBAL 20250903

article.story:
Chelsea forward Estêvão scored his first goal for Brazil in a 3-0 win over Chile in South American World Cup qualifying.

Lucas Paquetá and Bruno Guimarães were also on target as Brazil rose to second place in qualifying with just Wednesday's trip to Bolivia remaining.

The hosts dominated possession against the bottom team in South American World Cup qualifying, but had to wait until the final 20 minutes before wrapping up victory.

Manchester United midfielder Casemiro thought he had headed Brazil in front after just five minutes, only for an offside flag to cut short celebrations.

They continued to press, but had to wait until the 38th minute to take the lead with an acrobatic effort from Chelsea's Estêvão after Raphina's original effort was blocked.

"I've always dreamed of scoring my first goal for the national team, at the Maracanã, since I was a child," the 18-year-old forward said.

"I dedicate it to my family who are in London. We always come here and try our best to bring joy to the fans. "I was a huge fan of the national team, and playing today is a dream. I thank God, and now I just have to keep working to stay here, which is my dream.

"We played a great game, we managed to extend our lead. We can improve, we know that. We leave with a very positive feeling."

Chilean defender Guillermo Maripán was shown a red card before the break for a reckless challenge, but it was downgraded to a yellow after a VAR review.

West Ham midfielder Paqueta, who on Wednesday learned the Football Association would not be appealing an independent commission's decision to clear him of four charges of spot-fixing, came on as a substitute after 71 minutes and doubled the advantage within a minute.

A counter attack ended with Luiz Henrique crossing for Paqueta to head home, Newcastle's Guimaraes rolling the ball into an empty net four minutes later after Henrique's effort had been turned onto the bar.

Information from ESPN's Adriana Garcia contributed to this story.

videos.headline: Pedro: Playing for Chelsea and Brazil has always been my dream
videos.description: João Pedro says "it's the beginning of a beautiful story" after the 23-year-old earned a call-up to the Brazil squad.
